title: Firearms Ballistic Pathway
description: Annotated diagram of the three ballistic phases — internal, external, terminal — with clickable evidence items (rifling marks, breech face impression, GSR, trajectory angle, wound characteristics) that identify what each is, when it is produced, and how it is collected.
creator: Forensic Science Intelligent Textbook
subject: Forensic Science
Firearms Examination
Ballistics
Gunshot Residue
Wound Ballistics
bloomLevel: Remember (L1) — identify the evidence produced at each ballistic phase.

educational.topic: Ballistic phases and the evidence each produces
learningObjectives: Identify the three ballistic phases: internal, external, and terminal.
Match each phase to the evidence it produces (rifling marks, breech face impression, GSR, trajectory, wound morphology).
Describe what each evidence type is, when it forms, and how it is collected.

pedagogical.interactionPattern: Annotated clickable diagram: click an evidence label (or the entry/exit wound) to open a detail panel; hover the barrel cross-section to animate rifling striations
pedagogical.assessment: Students identify which phase produces each evidence item and read its collection procedure
pedagogical.modelType: Static annotated infographic with click-to-reveal detail panel
controls: click evidence labels and wound regions
